name: Wild Duck and Andouille Sauce Piquant
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown off the duck breasts in some olive oil.
2. Make a roux with the olive oil and flour (see Justin's recipe posted earlier).
3. Add onions, bell pepper, green onions, parsley, and garlic to the roux. Stir and cook until softened.
4. Gradually add water and stir well.
5. Add wine and more water, then seasonings (Louisiana hot sauce, salt, and cayenne) and tomato sauce. Mix thoroughly.
6. Incorporate the Andouille sausage or smoked sausage.
7. Add the duck breasts to the pot.
8. Simmer on low heat for 3 to 4 hours, stirring occasionally.
9. Adjust seasoning with additional salt and cayenne as desired.
10. Serve over spaghetti or rice.
Makes about 3 gallons, so this is ideal for a large gathering.
